old_claim.note: Concentric Castles provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Garrison logistics is an operating layer for defended castles and forts; fortification supplies the physical enclosure, walls, gates, and protected positions that the garrison supports.

source_shape.source_locator: Defend to the End activity says castles were defensive fortresses and lists curtain walls, gates, keeps, turrets, watchmen, wall-walk patrols, food supplies, water access, and garrison protection.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: English Heritage presents castle garrison operation as a defensive-fortress setting involving walls, guarded gates, patrol positions, supplies, and protected access to water.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports fortification as the physical operating substrate for garrison logistics. It does not support making the narrower concentric-castle design a prerequisite for all castle garrison support.

ontology_before: Castle Garrison Logistics depended on Concentric Castles, implying garrison support required the specialized concentric castle form.
ontology_after: Castle Garrison Logistics depends on broad early fortification infrastructure while leaving concentric castles as a specific later castle design, not a required logistics substrate.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: castle_garrison_logistics now depends on early_fortifications and no longer directly depends on concentric_castles.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed specifically to logistics inside concentric castles rather than castle garrison support generally.
A source showed food, arms, water, and watch logistics were uniquely dependent on concentric castle design.
The early_fortifications node were narrowed so it no longer covered defensive walls, protected enclosures, or fortified strongholds.
short_reason: Castle garrison logistics needs fortifications, not the concentric-castle subtype.
